meta.purpose: Crash-course flashcards for Element 5 (account activity / ongoing supervision), drafted before question-bank drilling. Every fact quoted verbatim from local corpus at drafting time. Coverage cross-checked against E5-batch-001.draft.json tested facts; all outcomes 5.1-5.16 covered, weighted toward 5.2, 5.6, 5.9, 5.13, 5.14.
meta.patch_note: Patch cards appended 2026-06-04 after open-cards solve gate identified missing facts (see qa-log.md).

cards.front: First-tier vs second-tier supervision: what is the second tier designed to do?
cards.back: Ensure no serious account issues were missed by first-tier supervision, and ensure first-tier supervision is conducted adequately. Different purpose; generally not the same depth.
cards.source_quote: "ensure no serious account issues were missed by ﬁrst-tier supervision, and ensure ﬁrst-tier supervision is conducted adequately." — gn-3900-20-001.txt ~line 266

cards.front: When may first-tier reviews be conducted centrally (head/regional office) instead of at each branch?
cards.back: If resources are available to conduct the review at that location AND the Dealer has adequate procedures for dealing with any issues identified.
cards.source_quote: "Dealers can conduct these reviews in a central location (head oDce or regional oDce) provided: there are resources available to conduct the review at that location," — gn-3900-20-001.txt ~line 229

cards.front: Remote supervision: what control applies to a business location without a Resident Supervisor?
cards.back: An assigned outside Supervisor who visits periodically to review business conduct and legal compliance; P&Ps must specifically address those visits.
cards.source_quote: "Business locations without Resident Supervisors should have an assigned outside Supervisor. The outside Supervisor should visit the location periodically to review business conduct and compliance with applicable laws." — gn-3900-20-001.txt ~line 169

cards.front: Account supervision P&Ps must specifically address which three dealer obligations (3926(2))?
cards.back: Identify clients presenting high risk to the Dealer Member; identify clients presenting high risk of improper securities-market activities; comply with all AML and terrorist financing requirements.
cards.source_quote: "(i) identify clients that present a high risk to the Dealer Member, (ii) identify clients that present a high risk of conducting improper activities in the securities markets" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8099

cards.front: Per CIRO guidance, which client profiles present a higher risk of improper market activity?
cards.back: Those with access to material non-public information about issuers; holders of control blocks of public issuers; market professionals.
cards.source_quote: "such as those who have access to material non-public information about issuers, holders of control blocks of public issuers and market professionals." — gn-3900-20-001.txt ~line 200

cards.front: What must a dealer's P&Ps include regarding client records (3926(5))?
cards.back: Controls for accessing and amending client records — untracked, unrestricted editing of KYC/client data breaches this.
cards.source_quote: "A Dealer Member's policies and procedures must include controls for accessing and amending client records." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8111

cards.front: Who approves account supervision P&Ps and any substantive amendments?
cards.back: The Dealer Member's Chief Compliance Officer or another appropriate Executive — not CIRO, not the branch Supervisor.
cards.source_quote: "any substantive amendments to such policies and procedures, must be approved by the Dealer Member's Chief Compliance Officer or another appropriate Executive." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8105

cards.front: What two written items must a dealer provide all supervisory staff (3926(4))?
cards.back: Procedures to be followed in reviewing account activity; confirmation of the dealer's expectations of supervisory staff regarding their supervisory roles and responsibilities.
cards.source_quote: "(i) procedures to be followed in reviewing account activity, and (ii) confirmation of the Dealer Member's expectations of supervisory staff," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8108

cards.front: What does IDPC 3927(3) require for Approved Persons with a history of regulatory infractions or questionable conduct?
cards.back: The dealer must establish and follow procedures implementing additional supervisory measures applicable to those Approved Persons — prior discipline increases, not discharges, supervision.
cards.source_quote: "procedures for the implementation of additional supervisory measures applicable to Approved Persons with a history of regulatory infractions or questionable conduct." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8131

cards.front: Name the two strict (enhanced) supervisory measures the syllabus lists for infraction-history Approved Persons.
cards.back: Pre-approval of orders by the Supervisor; daily and monthly review of ALL the AP's client accounts.
cards.source_quote: "Pre-approval of orders by Supervisor • Daily and monthly review of all client accounts" — syllabus-raw.txt ~line 1110

cards.front: What specific OEO risk must trade-review P&Ps address under 3955(2)?
cards.back: The risks associated with the method of order entry and the absence of intermediation by employees of the Dealer Member.
cards.source_quote: "must specifically address the risks associated with the method of order entry and the absence of intermediation by employees of the Dealer Member." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8267

cards.front: What supervision records and resources must the OEO entity maintain (3955(3)-(4))?
cards.back: An audit trail of all supervisory reviews as required in Rule 3900; sufficient supervisory resources allocated at head office and branch levels.
cards.source_quote: "must maintain an audit trail of all supervisory reviews as required in Rule 3900." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8269

cards.front: Two conditions before placing a temporary hold for financial exploitation of a vulnerable client?
cards.back: Dealer reasonably believes BOTH: the client is a vulnerable client, AND financial exploitation has occurred, is occurring, has been attempted or will be attempted.
cards.source_quote: "must not place a temporary hold on the basis of financial exploitation of a vulnerable client, unless the Dealer Member reasonably believes all of the following: (i) the client is a vulnerable client," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 4925

cards.front: After placing a temporary hold, what must the dealer do every 30 days until it ends?
cards.back: Within 30 days and each subsequent 30-day period: either revoke the hold, or give the client notice of the decision to continue it plus reasons.
cards.source_quote: "within 30 days of placing the temporary hold and, until the hold is revoked, within every subsequent 30‐day period," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 4947

cards.front: TRAP: temporary hold for lack of mental capacity — does it require a doctor's certificate?
cards.back: No. Standard is the dealer's reasonable belief the client lacks mental capacity to make decisions involving financial matters — a separate ground from exploitation.
cards.source_quote: "unless the Dealer Member reasonably believes that the client does not have the mental capacity to make decisions involving financial matters." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 4937

cards.front: 3945(2) retail detection list — recite the first six items (suitability/conduct cluster).
cards.back: Unsuitable trading; undue concentration (single account or across accounts); excessive trading; restricted-securities/derivatives trading; AP-client trading conflict of interest; excessive transfers and cancellations indicating possible unauthorized trading.
cards.source_quote: "(i) unsuitable trading, (ii) undue concentration of securities, derivatives or precious metals bullion in a single account or across accounts, (iii) excessive trading," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8145

cards.front: 3945(2) retail detection list — items (vii)-(xi) (pattern/conduct cluster)?
cards.back: Inappropriate or high-risk trading strategies; deterioration of quality of client holdings; excessive or improper crosses between clients; improper or excessive employee trading; front running.
cards.source_quote: "(vii) inappropriate or high risk trading strategies, (viii) deterioration of the quality of client holdings in an account," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8155

cards.front: 3945(2) retail detection list — final six items (administrative/market-abuse cluster)?
cards.back: Account number changes; late payment; outstanding margin calls; undisclosed short sales; manipulative and deceptive activities; insider trading.
cards.source_quote: "(xii) account number changes, (xiii) late payment, (xiv) outstanding margin calls, (xv) undisclosed short sales, (xvi) manipulative and deceptive activities, and" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8161

cards.front: Daily review red flag: what pattern signals possible UNAUTHORIZED trading?
cards.back: Excessive transaction transfers and trade cancellations — moving or unwinding trades hides activity the client never authorized.
cards.source_quote: "excessive transaction transfers and trade cancellations indicating possible unauthorized trading," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8153

cards.front: Churning (excessive trading): why won't single-trade review catch it, and what does guidance prescribe?
cards.back: Patterns may not be apparent in single trades; reviewing trades over a longer period can detect overall-activity issues even if each trade is suitable.
cards.source_quote: "If a Dealer reviews trades over a longer period, they may detect issues with the overall account activity even if each trade is suitable for the client." — gn-3900-20-001.txt ~line 191

cards.front: TRAP: fee-based retail accounts — can review selection be based on commission levels?
cards.back: No. P&Ps for accounts where no commission is charged must address review requirements AND use criteria other than commission levels.
cards.source_quote: "such as fee based accounts. These policies and procedures must: (i) address account activity review Corporation requirements, and (ii) use criteria other than commission levels." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8176

cards.front: Which five retail account types must be specifically designated for supervision purposes (3945(4))?
cards.back: Non-client accounts; discretionary accounts; managed accounts; registered accounts; restricted accounts.
cards.source_quote: "(i) non-client accounts, (ii) discretionary accounts, (iii) managed accounts, (iv) registered accounts, and (v) restricted accounts." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8182

cards.front: Expected completion timing: first-tier DAILY review vs first-tier MONTHLY review?
cards.back: Daily: one business day after the trading activity occurs. Monthly: within 21 days of the review period — both unless precluded by unusual circumstances.
cards.source_quote: "Dealers should complete this review within 21 days of the review period, unless precluded by unusual circumstances." — gn-3900-20-001.txt ~line 254

cards.front: TRAP: which non-client accounts go into the first-tier MONTHLY review?
cards.back: ALL non-client accounts with any activity other than dividend and interest payments — not just high-commission ones. ($3,000 belongs to second-tier monthly client criteria.)
cards.source_quote: "A ﬁrst-tier monthly review should include all non-client accounts with any activity other than dividend and interest payments." — gn-3900-20-001.txt ~line 251

cards.front: Guidance example selection thresholds: first-tier monthly vs second-tier monthly?
cards.back: First-tier monthly: clients charged gross commissions of $1,500+ that month. Second-tier monthly: clients charged $3,000+, plus $1,500+ accounts not subject to first-tier review.
cards.source_quote: "a Dealer could review the activity of all clients charged gross commissions of $1,500 or more for that month." — gn-3900-20-001.txt ~line 249

cards.front: Beyond trades, which four client-related matters must P&Ps identify, deal with, and inform Supervisors of (3946(1))?
cards.back: Client complaints; cash account violations; transfers between unrelated accounts or between non-client and client accounts (incl. deposits); trading while under-margined.
cards.source_quote: "(i) client complaints, (ii) cash account violations, (iii) transfers of funds and positions between unrelated accounts or between non-client accounts and client accounts" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8191

cards.front: Name six example second-tier DAILY review criteria from CIRO guidance.
cards.back: Stock trades >$5,000 priced under $5.00; stock trades >$20,000 at/over $5.00; bond trades >$100,000; non-client trading; producing branch managers' client accounts; trade cancellations.
cards.source_quote: "stock trades with a value over $5,000 and a price under $5.00 per share, stock trades with value over $20,000 and a price at or over $5.00 per share," — gn-3900-20-001.txt ~line 279

cards.front: Retail debt trade (non-primary-market): what remuneration disclosure must the confirmation contain?
cards.back: Either the total mark-up/mark-down, commission or other service charges applied, or total commission plus prescribed notice that dealer remuneration was added/deducted.
cards.source_quote: "the total amount of any mark-up or mark-down, commission or other service charges the Dealer Member applied to the transaction," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 7609

cards.front: Cross-supervision: a dually-registered Supervisor also trades for their own clients. Who reviews their client activity?
cards.back: Another Supervisor must review that Supervisor's advice to and trades for clients — no one supervises themselves.
cards.source_quote: "where a Supervisors is dually-registered and also advises or trades for their own clients, another Supervisor reviews that Supervisor's advice to and trades for clients." — gn-3900-20-001.txt ~line 148

cards.front: Which second-tier daily criterion plugs the producing-branch-manager self-review gap?
cards.back: 'Producing branch managers' client accounts' (plus all client accounts not reviewed by a branch manager) — the second tier catches what the producing manager cannot independently review.
cards.source_quote: "producing branch managers' client accounts, all client accounts not reviewed by a branch manager," — gn-3900-20-001.txt ~line 283

cards.front: Hold-mail procedures for retail clients — three minimum provisions (3219(1))?
cards.back: Written client authorization to hold mail; time limit — no longer than six months in any 12-month period; control and regular review of hold-mail accounts by a Supervisor.
cards.source_quote: "a requirement that limits the length of time that a "hold mail" order may remain in force for no longer than six months, in any 12 month period," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 4883

cards.front: TRAP: can a hold-mail order ever exceed six months?
cards.back: Yes, if: permitted by the dealer's P&Ps; P&Ps specifically address close supervision of such accounts; AND the appropriate Supervisor pre-approves the extended period.
cards.source_quote: "(ii) the Dealer Member has policies and procedures that specifically address the close supervision of such accounts, and (iii) the appropriate Supervisor pre‐approves the extended period." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 4892

cards.front: Returned mail for retail clients: two minimum procedural rules (3219(3))?
cards.back: Control and investigation by a person independent of the sales function (may sit in a business location); a record of all investigations and their results maintained.
cards.source_quote: "a rule requiring the control and investigation by a person independent of the sales function, but may be located within a business location," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 4897

cards.front: What must institutional account supervision P&Ps outline besides detection (3950(1))?
cards.back: The actions to deal with problems or issues identified from supervisory reviews — detection alone is not enough; remediation must be specified.
cards.source_quote: "These policies and procedures must outline the actions to deal with problems or issues identified from supervisory reviews." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8225

cards.front: 3950(2) institutional detection list — recite all seven items.
cards.back: Manipulative/deceptive activities; securities on the restricted list; derivatives with restricted-list underlying; front running by employee/proprietary accounts; transfer-restricted securities; derivatives with transfer-restricted underlying; exceeding derivative position/exercise limits.
cards.source_quote: "(i) manipulative and deceptive activities, (ii) trading in securities on the Dealer Member's restricted list," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8231

cards.front: Institutional front-running detection (3950(2)(iv)): whose accounts does the rule target?
cards.back: Front running by EMPLOYEE or PROPRIETARY accounts — trading ahead of institutional client orders through the firm's own or staff accounts.
cards.source_quote: "front running by employee or proprietary accounts," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8235

cards.front: TRAP: is 'unsuitable trading' or 'churning' on the institutional 3950(2) detection list?
cards.back: No — those are retail 3945(2) items. The institutional list is market-integrity focused: manipulation, restricted lists, front running, transfer restrictions, position/exercise limits.
cards.source_quote: "specifically address detecting improper or suspicious account activity including: (i) manipulative and deceptive activities," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8230

cards.front: A second-tier reviewer of institutional activity lacks authority to act on findings. What must procedures provide?
cards.back: Procedures for referring the identified issues to a Supervisor who has the appropriate authority.
cards.source_quote: "the Dealer should have procedures for referring these issues to a Supervisor who has the appropriate authority." — gn-3900-20-001.txt ~line 270

cards.front: TRAP: 'trading activity around margin/reduced margin rates and price thresholds' appears in which detection list?
cards.back: Syllabus 5.9's INSTITUTIONAL improper-activity list — a syllabus-only addition beyond the seven 3950(2) rule items.
cards.source_quote: "Trading activity around margin/reduced margin rates and price thresholds" — syllabus-raw.txt ~line 1178

cards.front: Define a 'direct electronic access account' (IDPC 1201).
cards.back: Account not subject to suitability determination where: client has DEA per NI 23-103; dealer provides no recommendations; dealer complies with UMIR DEA requirements and NI 23-103.
cards.source_quote: "the client has been provided with direct electronic access within the meaning of National Instrument 23-103," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 334

cards.front: DEA accounts and suitability sections 3402/3403 — fully exempt, or not quite?
cards.back: Almost — exempt except clauses 3402(3)(i) and 3403(4)(i): the determination that CONTINUING to have the account is suitable still applies.
cards.source_quote: "Other than clauses 3402(3)(i) and 3403(4)(i), sections 3402 or 3403 do not apply in respect to: (i) an order execution only account, or (ii) a direct electronic access account." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 5643

cards.front: OEO P&Ps: a client also has a full-service account at the dealer. May the OEO unit give recommendations?
cards.back: No — P&Ps must ensure clients are not provided recommendations as a result of having an account with a separate legal entity, separate business unit, or the dealer itself.
cards.source_quote: "ensure that clients are not provided with recommendations as a result of the client having an account with: (a) a separate legal entity of the Dealer Member," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8252

cards.front: OEO accounts are exempt from suitability — so what trading review scope still applies?
cards.back: Review customer trading and accounts for ALL Rule 3900 concerns other than those relating to the suitability requirements (manipulation, restricted lists, margin, etc.).
cards.source_quote: "to review customer trading and accounts for those concerns listed in Rule 3900, other than those relating to the suitability requirements." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8257

cards.front: OEO accounts offering OTC derivatives (e.g., CFDs) to retail clients: what extra disclosure accompanies the risk disclosure statement?
cards.back: The percentage of such accounts that were profitable for each of the four most recent quarters.
cards.source_quote: "a disclosure of the percentage of such accounts that were profitable for each of the four most recent quarters." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 5298

cards.front: A dealer transacts in both options and futures/CFDs. How must designated derivatives supervision be structured (3960(1))?
cards.back: Appoint, where applicable: a designated Supervisor for options contracts or similar derivatives, AND one for futures, forwards, CFDs, futures contract options or similar derivatives.
cards.source_quote: "appoint a designated Supervisor to supervise its activities that involve futures contracts, forward contracts, contracts for difference, futures contract options or similar derivatives." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8281

cards.front: TRAP: designated vs ALTERNATE derivatives Supervisor — when must the alternate step in (3960(3)-(4))?
cards.back: Alternates are appointed to ensure CONTINUOUS supervision; they must assume duties when the designated Supervisor is absent/unable, or when trading activity needs additional qualified supervisors.
cards.source_quote: "(i) the relevant designated Supervisor is absent or unable to carry out their duties, or (ii) a Dealer Member's trading activity requires additional qualified individuals" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8288

cards.front: Designated derivatives Supervisors are responsible for which two things (3961)?
cards.back: Approving new derivatives accounts; ensuring the handling of clients' derivatives account trading complies with Corporation requirements.
cards.source_quote: "(i) approving new derivatives accounts, and (ii) ensuring that the handling of clients' derivatives account trading complies with Corporation requirements." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8293

cards.front: 3963(1) retail derivatives detection — first five items (margin/limits cluster)?
cards.back: Excessive intra-day and short-term transactions; transacting while under-margined; transacting beyond margin or credit limits; cumulative losses exceeding approved client loss limits; exceeding derivative position or exercise limits.
cards.source_quote: "(i) excessive intra-day and short-term transactions, (ii) transacting while the account is under-margined, (iii) transacting beyond margin or credit limits," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8341

cards.front: 3963(1) retail derivatives detection — items (vi)-(xi) (conduct/exposure cluster)?
cards.back: Speculative transactions in hedging accounts; derivatives with restricted-list underlying; underlying with transfer restrictions; derivatives used to avoid insider trading restrictions; uncovered option exposures; futures/forwards held into the delivery month.
cards.source_quote: "(vi) speculative transactions in hedging accounts, (vii) transactions in derivatives whose underlying interest is on the Dealer Member's restricted list," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8347

cards.front: TRAP: 3950(2) institutional list vs 3963(1) retail derivatives list — where do 'speculative transactions in hedging accounts' and 'uncovered option exposures' belong?
cards.back: Both are 3963(1) RETAIL derivatives items only. Shared with the institutional list: restricted-list derivatives, transfer-restricted underlyings, position/exercise limits.
cards.source_quote: "(x) exposures arising out of uncovered option positions, and" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8359

cards.front: TRAP: which derivatives account type is carved out of the cumulative loss-limit regime?
cards.back: Hedging accounts. The limit otherwise applies whether the account is advisory, discretionary, managed or order execution only.
cards.source_quote: "applies to an account, other than a hedging account, whether the account is an advisory account, a discretionary account, a managed account or an order execution only account," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 5261

cards.front: How is a client's cumulative loss limit determined and maintained (3252(2)(iii))?
cards.back: Either on a lifetime basis validated with the client annually, or on an annual basis updated annually.
cards.source_quote: "(a) a lifetime basis and validated with the client on an annual basis, or (b) an annual basis and updated annually." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 5265

cards.front: TRAP: must every retail derivatives account be reviewed daily? Which ones MUST be reviewed daily AND monthly?
cards.back: No — accounts are selected using criteria giving reasonable assurance of detecting improper trading. But the designated Supervisor must review ALL derivatives discretionary and managed accounts daily and monthly.
cards.source_quote: "On a daily and monthly basis, the designated Supervisor must review all derivatives accounts that are designated as discretionary accounts and managed accounts." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8314

cards.front: Retail derivatives client notifications — P&Ps must address notifying clients of which four events (3962(4))?
cards.back: Approaching expiry dates; significant changes in derivatives from changes in the underlying interest; changes in the dealer's business policy; new developments in derivatives trading or regulation impacting clients.
cards.source_quote: "(i) approaching expiry dates, (ii) significant changes in derivatives resulting from changes in the underlying interest, (iii) any changes in the Dealer Member's business policy," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8318

cards.front: Retail CFD float cap: what intra-day/short-term position size must P&Ps prevent?
cards.back: A retail client holding CFD or similar derivative positions representing more than 0.5% of the float of a publicly traded issuer, on an intra-day or short-term basis.
cards.source_quote: "prevent a retail client from holding contracts for difference or similar derivatives positions representing more than 0.5% of the float of a reporting issuer" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8331

cards.front: TRAP: 0.5%-of-float cap vs the CFD product prohibition — which clause bans WHAT?
cards.back: 3962(5)(v) caps position SIZE at 0.5% of float. 3962(5)(vi) categorically prohibits retail CFDs conferring rights/obligations to acquire or deliver the underlying or shareholder rights (e.g., voting).
cards.source_quote: "prohibit the offering of contracts for difference or similar derivatives to a retail client that confer the right or obligation to acquire or deliver the underlying interest" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8334

cards.front: Before soliciting clients to use derivatives programs, what approval do P&Ps require? And before any transaction?
cards.back: Designated Supervisor must approve solicitation of clients to use derivatives programs AND clients' actual use of derivatives; no transacting without an executed derivatives trading agreement.
cards.source_quote: "require the designated Supervisor to approve the solicitation of clients to use derivatives programs, as well as clients' actual use of derivatives," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8323

cards.front: Besides solicitation approval and the trading agreement, name the other two 'address' items in 3962(5).
cards.back: Handling of futures, forwards and similar derivatives with pending delivery months; detection of derivatives trading by an insider client to avoid insider trading restrictions.
cards.source_quote: "address detection of derivatives trading by a client who is an insider of a reporting issuer or any other issuer whose securities are publicly traded to avoid insider trading restrictions," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8329

cards.front: What access must derivatives clients have during normal business hours (3964)?
cards.back: Access to an RR, IR, PM or APM qualified to deal in the applicable derivatives — options-type, futures/forwards/CFDs-type, or all derivatives.
cards.source_quote: "derivatives clients have access, during normal business hours, to a Registered Representative, Investment Representative, Portfolio Manager or Associate Portfolio Manager qualified to deal in," — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8365

cards.front: Managed account supervision P&Ps must specifically address which two matters (3971(2))?
cards.back: Identifying when a PM or sub-adviser has contravened the section 3280 managed-account conflict-of-interest requirements; ensuring fairness in allocating investment opportunities among managed accounts.
cards.source_quote: "ensuring fairness in the allocation of investment opportunities among its managed accounts." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8416

cards.front: TRAP: Associate Portfolio Manager advice to managed accounts — pre-approval or next-day review?
cards.back: PRE-approval: the APM is prohibited from providing advice unless a Portfolio Manager at the dealer approved it BEFORE the APM provides it. (Next-day review belongs to discretionary orders.)
cards.source_quote: "including a prohibition on the Associate Portfolio Manager providing advice unless the advice has been approved by a Portfolio Manager at the Dealer Member prior" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8420

cards.front: Managed account committee: minimum composition and annual duties (3972)?
cards.back: Includes at least one designated Supervisor responsible for managed accounts plus the CCO; at least annually reviews managed-account supervision P&Ps and recommends compliance actions to senior management.
cards.source_quote: "a managed account committee that includes at least one designated Supervisor responsible for managed accounts and the Chief Compliance Officer. The committee must, at least annually:" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8430

cards.front: TRAP: which supervisory reviews can the designated Supervisor NOT delegate, and how often is each managed account reviewed?
cards.back: Discretionary-account monthly financial-performance reviews (3970(1)-(2)) cannot be delegated to any other person; each managed account is reviewed QUARTERLY (objectives pursued; compliant handling).
cards.source_quote: "must not delegate the performance of the reviews required in subsections 3970(1) and 3970(2) to any other person." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8384

cards.front: A Supervisor resolves a flagged trade by phone and records nothing. What does 3927(2) require?
cards.back: Record and keep evidence of completed supervisory reviews, including details of inquiries about issues and their resolution, for the section 3803 retention period.
cards.source_quote: "must record and keep evidence of completed supervisory reviews, including details of inquiries about issues and their resolution, for the period required in section 3803." — idpc-rules.txt ~line 8129

cards.front: Default retention period for dealer records, including supervision documentation?
cards.back: Minimum seven years from the date the record is created, in durable and accessible form, unless a specific requirement prescribes differently.
cards.source_quote: "in durable and accessible form, for a minimum of seven years from the date the record is created unless Corporation requirements or securities laws" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 6721

cards.front: How often must a Participant's automated order systems be tested?
cards.back: Before use and at least annually thereafter, assuming various market conditions.
cards.source_quote: "and at least annually thereafter, each automated order system is satisfactorily tested assuming" — umir.txt ~line 7005

cards.front: Besides testing, what real-time controls must automated order systems have?
cards.back: Pre-programmed internal parameters that prevent or flag, in real time, orders/trades exceeding volume, order, price or other limits.
cards.source_quote: "internal parameters to prevent or “flag” with alerts on a real-time basis, the entry of orders and" — umir.txt ~line 7008

cards.front: What 'kill switch' capability does UMIR Policy 7.1 require for automated order systems?
cards.back: The Participant or Access Person must be able to immediately override or automatically disable the system, preventing its orders reaching any marketplace.
cards.source_quote: "Each Participant or Access Person must have the ability to immediately override or disable" — umir.txt ~line 7011

cards.front: TRAP: may OEO clients connect their own automated order systems?
cards.back: No — OEO clients must not use their own automated order system to generate/send orders or send orders on a pre-determined basis.
cards.source_quote: "use their own automated order system, as defined in securities laws, to generate" — idpc-rules.txt ~line 4986

cards.front: Whose risk controls govern a DEA client's orders sent to a marketplace?
cards.back: The Participant's: every order remains subject to its risk management and supervisory controls, policies and procedures, including automated controls.
cards.source_quote: "the order is subject to the risk management and supervisory controls, policies and procedures established by the Participant including the automated controls to examine each order before entry on a marketplace." — umir.txt ~line 8927
